reviews_data.review_text: Very busy, nice location live music was good.  Took a while to get beverages at bar while some tents were dead.

Don't. Just, don't.
Sleazy place, won't take cash that says legal tender for all debts, your going to be paying for parking, and other reviews say-

"biggest negative is the 3% service fee added to your bill without any kind of forewarning or explanation.  I'm not sure what this is for, I'm assuming for using a credit card.  I'm fine if places either have it posted, or tell you that there is a fee to use a credit card as I understand the associated fees with using a credit card.  But this fee was added without any indication or explanation.  Left a bad taste in my mouth, over a couple bucks.  I would like it if Monday Night Brewing was a little more transparent with their "3% Service Fee".
The responding post to this message said they'll remove this charge if you ask. Sounds perfectly legit to me.

I went there with an open mind as one of our group picked it. Not going back.
🤮
Awesome stop on the Lazoom tour!!
Great atmosphere, beer and food!

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Steve, thank you for taking the time to leave Monday Night Garden Co. your feedback. I want to apologize for your recent negative experience. Hospitality is a cornerstone to our culture at Monday Night and I'm sad to hear that you didn't receive the five star experience I know our team can deliver. I also understand your disappointment with the lack of live music. We are working hard to book more live music acts every weekend - unfortunately we have no control over whether the bands show up. We do, however, have control over our communication around these events and we will do better at communicating to our guests when cancellations and amendments are made through our social and other digital channels. I hope that you'll give us another shot to rectify the situation. Please email eden@mondaynightbrewing.com so that we can discuss your visit and how we can ensure your next one is worthy of five stars.
reviews_data.review_text: We went there while visiting for the Craft Brewery 5 Miler and Half marathon because thier website advised they had live music.  They Did not.  They were VERY busy so I get slow service but the Bartender (female) was the most rude service person I had ever come across. I get it, you are busy, but do't take it out out me, I am ONE (former) customer  There are way too many quality Breweries in CLT for me to decide to go back.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Rich, thank you for taking the time to leave Monday Night Garden Co. a five star review! We love when Atlanta transplants find our taprooms across the Southeast as a haven and a taste of home. We now have 6 locations spanning the Southeast including our two taprooms in Atlanta, and ones in Nashville, Birmingham, Knoxville, and Charlotte. Have you completed the whole Tour de Monday Night? Next time you're at Garden Co., try out the Hot Honey pizza, it's a personal and staff fave. Cheers!
reviews_data.review_text: Loved this place when I lived in  GA.  Always solid beer and their spot here in CLT is really nice with a lot on indoor and outdoor space.  Need to try the pizza next time.

Beers were good but food left much to be desired. Ordered an Italian sandwich and they were “ out of them”. Then ordeed a Peppadew pizza. Found one small bite in entire pizza that had all three toppings    In the bite. When went on pay noticed they added a 3% “optional” service charge and then the suggested tip amount includes a tip on top of the 3% surcharge. There were only four people in our party and there is no wait service so please explain the service charge!

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Kelly, thank you for taking the time to leave Monday Night Garden Co. your feedback. I want to apologize for your recent negative experience. We understand that long lines are frustrating but I promise you that our team is working hard behind the bar to keep our lines short - including helping multiple guests at a time. We want each guest to receive the same experience and hospitality that we provide and sometimes that can create periods of waiting. We are absolutely open to feedback though so if you have any, please email eden@mondaynightbrewing.com. We'd love if you gave us another shot and to provide an experience worthy of five stars.
reviews_data.review_text: Fix the long lines! As a brewery you  should have a line at the bar for beer only . no one should have to wait in line for 20 minutes for a bunch of clowns ordering espresso martinis

reviews_data.review_text:
Great spot with an awesome vibe. The pizza is really good, the atmosphere is relaxed, and the garden area is beautiful, perfect for hanging out with friends or enjoying a casual afternoon. The whole place has a fun, welcoming energy that makes you want to stay longer. Live Music on Saturdays too.

Five stars for good food, good vibes, and a garden space that really sets it apart.

The place has no parking. There is paid parking across both streets.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hey Iron Bulldog, thank you for taking the time to leave Monday Night Garden Co. your feedback. We're really sorry to hear that you didn't enjoy our pizzas. reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Peyton, thank you for taking the time to leave your feedback of Monday Night Garden Co. We absolutely do not condone discrimination at our taprooms and if you felt like you were treated differently than any other guest in our bar then I truly do apologize. reviews_data.review_text:
During our recent company event, my group and I experienced discriminatory treatment from the bartender. We were having a great time, being respectful, and simply enjoying ourselves during game night only to be refused service without notice or explanation.

We were later told we were “too loud,” which felt unjustified and targeted, especially considering it was a game night in a bar, an environment where energy and conversation are expected. The abrupt refusal to take our drink orders, without any prior warning or professionalism, came across as both dismissive and racially biased.

This was not only embarrassing but completely unacceptable, particularly as this was a company-sponsored event where we had already placed a large order earlier in the evening. We expect to be treated with the same respect and courtesy as any other paying guests.

I hope this issue is taken seriously and addressed immediately to ensure no other patrons experience the same kind of discriminatory behavior.

We fully intend to take this matter to the highest level necessary to ensure accountability and to prevent similar discriminatory behavior from occurring in the future.

We expect a written acknowledgment of this complaint and a formal response outlining the steps your establishment intends to take to address this incident.

reviews_data.review_text: Went there for the gluten freedom event, the  pizza and wings were delicious, didnt get to try the burgers because they weren't ready yet. That being said, wait times were long but I feel like it was to be expected as the lines were out the door and the food was freshly made.  Mabey next time some different systems could be put in place to accommodate the large turnout. Regardless,  cool bar, kids loved the pinball machines, and good pizza.

reviews_data.review_text: We weren't even aware that this brewery existed in Charlotte until I stumbled across a two day gluten-free pizza event they were doing. My wife is celiac and is not able to eat pizza out because of cross contamination, so the fact that Monday Night shut down for a day to completely clean the ovens and prep area for the event was amazing. The space is awesome, they have pool tables and a pinball room (including a Led Zeppelin III machine!), tons of seating both inside and out, the pizzas and beers were incredible, and the staff is phenomenal. We were VERY impressed with everything and we'll definitely go back even if they're not doing a gluten-free event. This was a great find and Monday Night is a wonderful addition to the Charlotte brewery scene!

We're sorry to hear about your recent bout of food discomfort. I can assure you that our house spirit, Sneaky Pete Vodka, is gluten free. As well as our house tequila, Pedro Furtivo. We'd love to explain the way we process our liquor and give you an insight on our liquor and gluten free programs. Please shoot us an email at eden@mondaynightbrewing.com so that we can get you set up for our next round of Gluten Freedom, May 13-14!
reviews_data.review_text:
Absolutely love this bar and is one of my absolute favorites. We love their gluten-free nights and have been to every one of them. Recently, I went in for their gf night and had the lavender lemonade, I usually get it with Tito’s but since it’s no longer on their menu with the listed ingredients, I didn’t even think of it. I had one sip of their sneaky Pete house vodka, and realized immediately and asked if they could remake it with Tito’s, which they were incredibly accommodating about.

It was entirely my fault as I know my allergy and how sensitive I am, even after alcohol is distilled. Moral of the story, I did get badly glutened, should you have celiac disease, avoid their house liquor and stick to what you know! Not knocking them at all, they rock and their GF nights are awesome. I just wanted to put out a psa for those who do often attend their gluten freedom nights to avoid getting glutened! Please keep in mind, I have never gotten sick from their pizza and cheesy bread for their gluten free nights, this was solely alcohol based!

reviews_data.review_text: Our beer and food were really good. We really enjoyed the old ale. We didn’t enjoy the atmosphere. This is a very large brewery and it was incredibly crowded. If you’re looking for a chill place for a good beer this isn’t it. I have zero complaints about the food or beer. I just disliked the loud techno music and flashing lights.

reviews_data.review_text: 6 tiny wings, all flats, not 1 drum, for $13? I've had wings at most every bar in Charlotte, these are the worst deal in town. Sauce wasn't bad.

Can you go to Monday Night Brewing on a Tuesday night? Yes, you can. Can you get Monday night beer on a Tuesday night? Yes, you can. Can you get an amazing pizza to share with your wife on a Monday night? Yes, you can.

Finishing appointments and ready to chill, we stopped here and lucked out with a good parking space. Inside, an after-work party was going on and we grabbed seats and a menu.

I placed our order at the bar - a beer for me, a "Hibiscus Flower Tea" for my wife and we shared the "Biancha Funghi" pizza. The pizza did not take long. Loaded and I mean LOADED with garlic and also marinated mushrooms and olives and fresh basil, this was "melt your paint" amounts of garlic. Oh, and with it the garlic aioli dipping sauce. Absolutely delicious and my lips were buzzing with garlic for hours.

A very large indoor space and lots of "beer garden" space outside too. Check out the "loot drop" and the ice cream vending machines while you're here plus a large room of pinball machines.

We recently hosted our wedding welcome party at Monday Night Brewing and we cannot recommend them enough for your next event!  My husband’s last name is Fox, so choosing Monday Night Brewing with their Sneaky Pete liquors and Yacht Waters seemed like a perfect fit.  When I tell you the staff went above and beyond for the “Fox” theme – Not only did they print out fox-themed cocktail menus AND re-named 5 of their cocktails with punny fox names (shout out to the Who Let the Fox Out) but they also had a Fox mascot come out during the party to take pictures with guests!  We had an absolute blast, the food was incredible, and all of the staff were so great.  We had so many compliments from guests throughout the weekend (who were mostly out of towners) about how perfect the space was and how much fun they had at Monday Night.  I cannot recommend Monday Night enough for your next event, and can’t wait for my next excuse to host a party with them!

A great local brewery with a fantastic variety. The Oatmeal Raisin Cookie quad was outstanding!! Try this brewery,  you'll definitely find something you like, including their delicious pizza. Highly recommended!

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Natalie, thank you for taking the time to leave Monday Night Garden Co. your feedback. While we believe our pizza tastes best while enjoyed fresh out our wood-fired ovens, that's no excuse for the take out that you experienced. reviews_data.review_text: I hate writing bad reviews especially since I’ve only ordered take out from here but there is no email listed to contact about quality issues, and the phone goes straight to voicemail because staff members aren’t available to answer the phone. Unfortunately came home to find that our food was only half cooked. We ordered 2 pizzas using toast and it was ready In less than 15 minutes which maybe should have been a red flag for stone fired pizza. When we got home and opened the boxes the pizzas immediately didn’t look as advertised- the crust wasn’t inflated and for $22 a pizza I guess I would have expected more. I ordered the marinara pizza and added Calabrian chilis and they put the whole chilis on the pizza instead of cutting them? A lot of heat to bite into but okay. Online the pizza says basil, there was no basil. We also ordered the funghi and added prosciutto. The prosciutto wasn’t even baked onto the pizza, it looked as if it had been plopped on there as an after thought. The dough was gummy and tasted undercooked. I had heard good things about the pizza so I was shocked to get the opposite experience. Maybe an isolated incident, but it’s not worth trying again and paying another $50 for 2 personal sized pizzas.

The pizza there is really delicious just eat it while it's hot. They have pinball ,pool , and a claw machine. The outside is also set up pretty nice if your are near by or looking for a place to have a hand crafted beer and delicious food stop on by and say hi to the very friendly staff come on by

reviews_data.review_text: I've reviewed many places all over the states and the world. This by far is the WORST experience out of them all. I just left there because if you have any melanin in your skin, you will not be acknowledged at the bar. Several patrons that didn't look like me were acknowledged and served after I was standing and patiently waiting. It was so bad that my wife and I left. I asked a gentleman if he could take my order, and he said someone else would. At this time, I asked to speak to a manager. The bar manager (manager on duty as the bartender claimed they didn't know where the manager was) was apologetic and asked to make it right by offering free drinks and pizza coupons. Here's the thing....at this point, I don't want anything from this establishment. I don't need anything free and typically will spend lots of money with businesses that act if they welcome all people. I will make sure I let everyone in my community and circle know. P.S. They come from many backgrounds, communities, and ethnicities. As a leader of DEIB initiatives within the community,  it's a shame that this occurred. This was our first time visiting and our last. We really wanted to like and try this place. If I could give a negative 1000 stars, I would.
I'm slightly biased but I love this place!  Great vibes, delicious beer, delicious pizza!

On the flip side, it is a bit expensive and parking is difficult, but the biggest negative is the 3% service fee added to your bill without any kind of forewarning or explanation. I'm fine if places either have it posted, or tell you that there is a fee to use a credit card as I understand the associated fees with using a credit card. I would like it if Monday Night Brewing was a little more transparent with their "3% Service Fee".

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Wellington, I'm so sorry that this happened to you and your family. We work with a third party security service and we're currently investigating your situation and working on a resolution to let our patrons with families know about our late night parameters. reviews_data.review_text:
My family and I are visiting Charlotte for a future relocation and this place was great. Awesome beer selection, pizzas and great place for kids to hang out.

However, we had a very uncomfortable incident with your head of security personnel at around 11PM. He became aggressive to our table saying that we can't be here anymore with kids past 10PM. We were totally unaware of this rule. Instead of coming to speak with us and let us know about this rule. This guy came up telling us we had to leave now and that kids can't be here. He grabbed me and tried to take me out of my chair and my wife told him we were leaving multiple times before he released me. I went to speak with management and my wife was walking out with her hand up telling him to take steps back because he was very aggressive and escalating a simple situation. He grabbed her arm and kept pushing towards her making her feel extremely uncomfortable especially with our daughter behind her. He continued to put his finger in her face and yelling at her even though she was just requesting space to feel comfortable to leave walking out. He continued to grab my wife's arm and pushing her outside of the property even though she was leaving but did not want to turn her back on this aggressive man. I tried speaking to the manager but he was physically pushing me out, assuming he didn't want to face repercussions of his actions.

We understand they security professionals, by standard, have to deescalate situation and he was definitely doing the opposite, even though we were telling him we were on our way out and leaving. He physically assaulted us and there are many witness to prove it.

It turned from a simple "kids are not allowed to stay here after 10PM" to "families get assaulted after 10PM"

We hope that this situation is addressed ASAP within your business so that other families don't go through what we went through.

We will also be filing a report with the local police.

reviews_data.review_text:
Absolutely love this bar and is one of my absolute favorites. We love their gluten-free nights and have been to every one of them. Recently, I went in for their gf night and had the lavender lemonade, I usually get it with Tito’s but since it’s no longer on their menu with the listed ingredients, I didn’t even think of it. I had one sip of their sneaky Pete house vodka, and realized immediately and asked if they could remake it with Tito’s, which they were incredibly accommodating about.

It was entirely my fault as I know my allergy and how sensitive I am, even after alcohol is distilled. Moral of the story, I did get badly glutened, should you have celiac disease, avoid their house liquor and stick to what you know! Not knocking them at all, they rock and their GF nights are awesome. I just wanted to put out a psa for those who do often attend their gluten freedom nights to avoid getting glutened! Please keep in mind, I have never gotten sick from their pizza and cheesy bread for their gluten free nights, this was solely alcohol based!

Can you go to Monday Night Brewing on a Tuesday night? Yes, you can. Can you get Monday night beer on a Tuesday night? Yes, you can. Can you get an amazing pizza to share with your wife on a Monday night? Yes, you can.

Finishing appointments and ready to chill, we stopped here and lucked out with a good parking space. Inside, an after-work party was going on and we grabbed seats and a menu.

I placed our order at the bar - a beer for me, a "Hibiscus Flower Tea" for my wife and we shared the "Biancha Funghi" pizza. The pizza did not take long. Loaded and I mean LOADED with garlic and also marinated mushrooms and olives and fresh basil, this was "melt your paint" amounts of garlic. Oh, and with it the garlic aioli dipping sauce. Absolutely delicious and my lips were buzzing with garlic for hours.

A very large indoor space and lots of "beer garden" space outside too. Check out the "loot drop" and the ice cream vending machines while you're here plus a large room of pinball machines.

We recently hosted our wedding welcome party at Monday Night Brewing and we cannot recommend them enough for your next event!  My husband’s last name is Fox, so choosing Monday Night Brewing with their Sneaky Pete liquors and Yacht Waters seemed like a perfect fit.  When I tell you the staff went above and beyond for the “Fox” theme – Not only did they print out fox-themed cocktail menus AND re-named 5 of their cocktails with punny fox names (shout out to the Who Let the Fox Out) but they also had a Fox mascot come out during the party to take pictures with guests!  We had an absolute blast, the food was incredible, and all of the staff were so great.  We had so many compliments from guests throughout the weekend (who were mostly out of towners) about how perfect the space was and how much fun they had at Monday Night.  I cannot recommend Monday Night enough for your next event, and can’t wait for my next excuse to host a party with them!

reviews_data.review_text: I hate writing bad reviews especially since I’ve only ordered take out from here but there is no email listed to contact about quality issues, and the phone goes straight to voicemail because staff members aren’t available to answer the phone. Unfortunately came home to find that our food was only half cooked. We ordered 2 pizzas using toast and it was ready In less than 15 minutes which maybe should have been a red flag for stone fired pizza. When we got home and opened the boxes the pizzas immediately didn’t look as advertised- the crust wasn’t inflated and for $22 a pizza I guess I would have expected more. I ordered the marinara pizza and added Calabrian chilis and they put the whole chilis on the pizza instead of cutting them? A lot of heat to bite into but okay. Online the pizza says basil, there was no basil. We also ordered the funghi and added prosciutto. The prosciutto wasn’t even baked onto the pizza, it looked as if it had been plopped on there as an after thought. The dough was gummy and tasted undercooked. I had heard good things about the pizza so I was shocked to get the opposite experience. Maybe an isolated incident, but it’s not worth trying again and paying another $50 for 2 personal sized pizzas.

reviews_data.review_text: I've reviewed many places all over the states and the world. This by far is the WORST experience out of them all. I just left there because if you have any melanin in your skin, you will not be acknowledged at the bar. Several patrons that didn't look like me were acknowledged and served after I was standing and patiently waiting. It was so bad that my wife and I left. I asked a gentleman if he could take my order, and he said someone else would. At this time, I asked to speak to a manager. The bar manager (manager on duty as the bartender claimed they didn't know where the manager was) was apologetic and asked to make it right by offering free drinks and pizza coupons. Here's the thing....at this point, I don't want anything from this establishment. I don't need anything free and typically will spend lots of money with businesses that act if they welcome all people. I will make sure I let everyone in my community and circle know. P.S. They come from many backgrounds, communities, and ethnicities. As a leader of DEIB initiatives within the community,  it's a shame that this occurred. This was our first time visiting and our last. We really wanted to like and try this place. If I could give a negative 1000 stars, I would.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Wellington, I'm so sorry that this happened to you and your family. We work with a third party security service and we're currently investigating your situation and working on a resolution to let our patrons with families know about our late night parameters. We would greatly appreciate any more details you have about your experience and we hope that you will give us another opportunity to welcome you and your family to Charlotte. Please email us at eden@mondaynightbrewing.com so that we can work towards a resolution together.
reviews_data.review_text:
My family and I are visiting Charlotte for a future relocation and this place was great. Awesome beer selection, pizzas and great place for kids to hang out.

However, we had a very uncomfortable incident with your head of security personnel at around 11PM. He became aggressive to our table saying that we can't be here anymore with kids past 10PM. We were totally unaware of this rule. Instead of coming to speak with us and let us know about this rule. This guy came up telling us we had to leave now and that kids can't be here. He grabbed me and tried to take me out of my chair and my wife told him we were leaving multiple times before he released me. I went to speak with management and my wife was walking out with her hand up telling him to take steps back because he was very aggressive and escalating a simple situation. He grabbed her arm and kept pushing towards her making her feel extremely uncomfortable especially with our daughter behind her. He continued to put his finger in her face and yelling at her even though she was just requesting space to feel comfortable to leave walking out. He continued to grab my wife's arm and pushing her outside of the property even though she was leaving but did not want to turn her back on this aggressive man. I tried speaking to the manager but he was physically pushing me out, assuming he didn't want to face repercussions of his actions.

We understand they security professionals, by standard, have to deescalate situation and he was definitely doing the opposite, even though we were telling him we were on our way out and leaving. He physically assaulted us and there are many witness to prove it.

It turned from a simple "kids are not allowed to stay here after 10PM" to "families get assaulted after 10PM"

We hope that this situation is addressed ASAP within your business so that other families don't go through what we went through.

We will also be filing a report with the local police.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thanks for the kind words. Our team is the best in Charlotte - make sure to ask for a recommendation for a new beer or cocktail to try.

reviews_data.review_text: Great variety of drinks! Beer, cocktails, their own distilled spirits! Decor was cute, and lots of sitting areas indoors and out. The outdoor area is huge. There’s also food on site- pizza. Great place to go and hang out for a long while.

reviews_data.review_text:

I got sick he didn’t. Came back today great great beer, an imperial stout and a double 🙌 however you’re going to charge me 17 bucks for a burnt small pizza with pepperoni and pickled onion. Beer and cocktails are expensive and so is the pizza but willing to pay more for great just have to deliver in all categories.

Also there is no parking so get over here early and walk. Overall good drinks and music are good. parking sucks, and pizza.. may the odds be in your favor

reviews_data.review_text: Monday Night Brewery in Charlotte offers an exceptional experience for patrons seeking top-notch cocktails and draft beer. The friendly staff ensures a warm welcome, and the establishment’s dog-friendly policy means you can enjoy your favorite drink without leaving your furry friend behind. reviews_data.review_text:
The atmosphere is always great here whether you're by yourself or with a group of friends. Yeah, parking can be a bit of a mess and the lot across the street will rip your heart out in a swift monetary fashion but once you get past those obstacles, it's smooth sailing from then on! I have been here a few times now and it has never disappointed me.

The pizza is close to as good as it's going to get for wood fired style pizza but if you indulge yourself in a couple beers, you'll probably be happy with pizza being in your presence anyways. During my last visit, I ordered the Peppadew but honestly I prefer the Margherita here as it's simple yet flavorful. Hopefully, this gives you the inside scoop but due to how busy this place typically is, you probably already knew the value this place has to offer!

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hey Shen, we are so thankful for such a nice review! It excites us to hear you had such a great time with us and we hope to have you again soon to help keep our vibes going!
reviews_data.review_text: Great service. Good pizza & salads. Good vibes. Lots of space and pet friendly!
Love the vibes in this place great food great beer and amazing people

Delicious pizza and great beers!! I love that they offer liquor and wine also if you’re not a beer drinkers. The whole vibe is amazing, complete with pool table and games. Amazing outdoor area, cool secluded room with groovy yellow windows, dog friendly all around. 10/10 recommend!

Such a fun environment and lots of space! Great music the night I went and the smell of pizza made it even better! The bartenders are quick and friendly and make great drinks! 10/10 recommend and will go back with all the friends!
My husband and I love Monday Night, the drinks are consistently good. I usually order their sour beers - they have a nice variety and I have not been disappointed!

Wow!!! Completely blown away by this place! A friend told me about how they completely clean top to bottom once a month to do a safe gluten free day. Being diagnosed with celiac about 5 years ago I never would have imagined I would ever be able to have wood fired (good) pizza again, wrong! This place is amazing! Haven’t found a pizza anywhere that is GF that tops this! You found a customer for life, thanks for making me feel normal again :)

Really great atmosphere with lots of outdoor and indoor/outdoor space.  Great place to bring the pups to take in the smells. Really solid wood-fired pizza and great beers. This place is buzzing most nights of the week with so many apartments nearby in Southend.
Great place, great drinks and friendly staff. The trivia nights are a great time!
Great drinks, love the outdoor seating!
Our second visit for paws pawty, this time pizza was great so as beer. Fun place to hang out with your dog.
One of my favorite places to hang out. Outside or inside there’s always plenty of seating and people to meet and hangout with! great drinks and food as well
Love this place! Great for meeting friends, and bringing your dogs!
The atmosphere was great and I loved the pizza!

My first time visiting Monday Night Brewing was for the Backyard Bazaar and that along with the brewery did not disappoint! I absolutely loved the atmosphere and the beer and hot honey pizza were delicious. I cannot wait to become a regular!
Awesome facility with great beer and pizza!
Great atmosphere and fantastic pizza. Highly recommend!

Went for the Backyard Bazaar (each month on the 3rd Friday I believe!) and even though it had been raining most of the day prior, it was packed and filled with great energy! Service was quick even though the bar was never not busy (sun time sangria 10/10!), the pizza was so yummy (highly recommend the hot honey!) and the live music was the perfect background volume and vibe. Can't wait to make this spot a regular one!
Great brewery with full bar (interesting cocktails), great pizza, and live music!
I always have a great experience here! Definitely a good place to go and relax and have a good time with friends!

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hi Kellen I want to apologize for this. Can you shoot us an email to Eden@mondaynightbrewing.com so we can try and make it right?
reviews_data.review_text:
Took an hour to get a burnt pizza.  There are other better breweries with better options so we won’t  be back

Update 4/21/24
Wow, the response from the company has been amazing. They are doing everything possible to make sure we try the pizza again.   We will be back next weekend and look forward to trying their pizza again.

Sprawling outdoor area, good food, great beer. Kid loves playing on the turf-covered hills. Parking can be a challenge during busy times but it’s not too bad. I’m sure Monday Night will be a Charlotte staple for many years.

My first time there and I had the pleasure of meeting Lauren! She’s phenomenal, pretty sure she made my drink taste better just by having her energy around! The location is perfect and the vibe is quite nice, which is rare because southend is now overpacked constantly. So coming here was refreshing and made me forget I was in a newly overcrowded city!
Great place to spend the night with friends. Had an awesome cocktail menu + lots of places to hang out
Alex was great.  She was amazing
Great pizza, great beer! Great for families or a night out! Enjoy myself every time I go!
Love the space! It's one of my favorite spots in Charlotte for pool and an ice cold Taco Tuesday!

Love the bartenders (Lauren, Amanda, Gwen all rule), love the vibe. One of the nicest outside areas of any breweries in the area in my opinion. The pizza is delicious, lots of different beers on tap, and non alcoholic options also! I’m not a cocktail person but would sacrifice my future first born for the pickle martini. 10/10!
Very dog friendly and great bartenders! Our favorite South End brewery!

I had the best experience at Monday Night! The food was absolutely delicious and despite how busy the venue was, every employee was working overtime to provide the quickest service. They were so polite and served a smile with every request. 10/10 experience!
Great vibes, great drinks, great staff, all sorts of events for every walk of life, and an amazing run club!

Thank you to Monday Night Brewery for hosting our Member Happy Hour! The venue was fantastic, offering a welcoming atmosphere and a great selection of brews. Our members had a wonderful time, and we look forward to returning in the future!
Sure great atmosphere

Great drink selection and wood fired pizza. Would recommend but the staff is the type to save a butterfly over a human. Ipad generation staff hitting the market.. Get ready for many communication breakdowns..

I have visited the Monday Night Brewing Garden Co. a few times. There is always a great group of people and a good atmosphere inside and out. They have a wide variety of drinks to choose from and pizza to order. Check it out, you won’t be disappointed!
My favorite beer of Charlotte! Always has amazing events
Dogs, pizzas, beers, cocktails, frequent events & a great patio!

Who knew Charlotte needed another brewery?? Monday night has good beer but even better cocktails and pizza. The lavender lemonade drink was one of the best I had last summer. Love their pizza with hot honey. Great vibes here and plenty to do. Happy with this addition to clt!
I loved the atmosphere and cocktails ! Such a great vibe !
Great environment and even better beer! Excited to go back.

Monday Night is right in our backyard and we couldn't be luckier, we love this place so much. The staff is so kind, especially the manager. She is always so receptive to feedback and goes above and beyond with her customers. Cocktails are delicious, environment is 10/10, pizzas are over priced for the size **in my opinion**. They also don’t offer gluten free crust which sucks for the gluten intolerant folk like myself! However, its still our fave brewery in Southend :)
Great food and drinks. The staff is amazing!

Loved Gluten Freedom day! Thank you for providing this great option.  The wood fired pizza was amazing.  The crust was perfect  and not Cauliflower - real crust! We had the Calabrian Pizza which had a great spiciness!  They also had a good gluten free beer.   Can't wait to return for the next Gluten freedom day!
We loved Monday Night Brewing! Family friendly, great food, and great drinks! Will definitely be back!
Love Monday Night Brewing. Perfect place to grab some drinks and kids have a blast too!
Great local spot with good vibes.

We visited Monday Night Brewing for the first time a week or so ago. It's a great space with lots of room. They have a little bit of everything to drink so if beer is not your thing, you have options. The pizza they make on site is very good. Looking forward to hanging out in their great outdoor space when it warms up.

Loved this place. I went for their Galentines day event!
However, I’m making this review to let everyone know that the parking lot google takes you is NOT parking for the brewery. It’s parking for the apartment complex behind the brewery. There’s street parking for the brewery but that’s about it.
Gwen is the best! Always a good time and very friendly! Super knowledgeable to help you make the best decision for your liquid encouragement.
The drinks are amazing and the pesto pizza is to dieeee forrrr!
Amazing!!
Delicious pizza!

Monday night is an amazing brewery that takes care of their customers. I was lucky enough to get to try a few of their brews at a trade show in Charlotte and have been in love ever since. So in love it has become a regular flagship in my Kegerator. Amanda and the staff are A+++ very kind and curious. Highly recommended! Cheers!
Great fun, awesome outdoor space. Went during one of their events, lots of incredible vendors and live music. Good food and drinks!
Good vibes, lots of seating and open spaces, good food and tasty beers!
Very unique and relaxed atmosphere. Love it!
Lots of fun with great beer!

This place is fantastic. Amazing beer (and also cocktails, mimosas, etc.), ample space both inside and outside. Great curated events schedule. Games.  I will most certainly be back here. The X11 stout is out of this world.

Dog friendly, delicious pizza, and great drinks! Definitely my favorite brewery in CLT. Love the atmosphere and outdoor space as well. They often have live music and DJ’s that enhance the overall experience. We always have a great time when we visit. Highly recommend.
Great brewery, great service and a fun welcoming environment!

In the South End neighborhood of Charlotte, Monday Night Brewing is A nice place for some casual beers or cocktails with VERY GOOD Neapolitan style pizza. They have many different spaces inside and out in which for you to sit. Service was prompt. The bread/crust on the pizza was really excellent. I would return…. A good casual brewery with good food to snack on. Surprisingly close to the light rail as well, which is a plus.
Great beer, pizza and staff.  This will be my new go to spot, their dark beer is divine.
Great service, great beers, cool atmosphere. We enjoyed the pool table room.
Large brewery with lots of room, delicious pizza, and dog-friendly! We enjoy coming here.
Always a good time- family friendly with a hill our daughter loves and great beer and food.
Mitch is the best. All there is to it.
Knowledgeable, professional and friendly bartenders. Large facility and lots of outdoor space.
So fun and super hopping for a Monday. Lots of fire pits outdoors, and such a great atmosphere (dog friendly!). Bar service was quick and the pizza was so tasty.

Monday Night Brewing’s staff is incredible. Yes, the beer is very good, but the way they treat me and Walt (my golden retriever) is even better. It’s such a joy to visit the location with how they make me feel as a local. I highly recommend them to anyone in the area.
Love the atmosphere here! Tons of seating and lots of different drink options. Bartenders are knowledgeable about the menu and give great recommendations!
Great spot with an amazing outdoor area.

My gf and I wanted to check this place out so we went for the first time last night. We ordered drinks and my gf said the bartender was a little rude to her, but I wasn’t paying much attention and brushed it off as a misunderstanding. When closing out the same bartender (the girl with the pimple patch) was very rude to me this time, and for no apparent reason. I should say the guy bartender was perfectly nice and the venue is huge and well laid out and the beer was fine. If I could rate everything else highly and the ornery bartender poorly I would, but unfortunately she was part of the experience. Won’t be going back.
2$ for ranch? where am I?? how much do you have to bleed me dry for this dipping sauce man?

Monday Night is a great addition to the Brewery scene in Charlotte. I love stouts and barleywines…they do a great job with theirs. And truly, you can go there simply for the food. The Calabrian pizza and the arugula salad are wonderful. Wood fired pizza and a great indoor outdoor layout make it a beautiful option. Let’s not forget the full bar. My wife’s martini was “delish” per her. Southend continues to grow with good options but this one is top notch.

Great spot for drinks. Their beer is good, but the creative cocktails are what really shine. A bit loud/crowded at times, but there’s enough seating to get away from noise even if you have a big group.
Monday Night provides great vibes and a friendly/welcoming atmosphere. The pizza is great and the beer even better. It has a large open space inside and out and the guests are usually very laid back.

Great brewery with lots of weekly events and solid beer. Love the vibe with lots of games and outdoor space and different seating areas. It's across the street from my gym so my partner and I go more than we probably should!
Expensive beer for the run of mill lagers and ales so went for the dark. Mexican hot chocolate was good and spiced. Tasteful live music
Mitchy is BOMB!  The  best guy ever!  Excellent!
Gluten freedom. Fantastic. Thank you, my celiac gf loved it.

A really cool venue with a lot of outdoor space. I just came by for one beer and it was very good. Just a side note: parking was very limited. I definitely plan to come back and will probably take the train to get there.
Great vibes, great beer and amazing pizza! Love bringing my out of town friends here as the location is amazing too!
Went here for the first time last night and it was amazing!! Fun drinks, great music, and great pizza
Great atmosphere
Great service and the outdoor seating is awesome. Great beer too
Great beer, food & atmosphere!
